perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information does not include information, technical data or know -how which: (i) is in the possessio n of the receiving party at the time of disclosure as shown by the receiving party's files and records immediately prior to the time of disclosure; (ii) prior or after the time of disclosure becomes part of the public knowledge or literature, not as a resu lt of any inaction or action of the receiving party; or (iii) is approved for release by the Discloser in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information does not include information, technical data or know -how which: (i) is in the possessio n of the receiving party at the time of disclosure as shown by the receiving party's files and records immediately prior to the time of disclosure; (ii) prior or after the time of disclosure becomes part of the public knowledge or literature, not as a resu lt of any inaction or action of the receiving party; (iii) is approved for release by the Discloser in writing; or (iv) becomes known to the Recipient through observation.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation adds a clause stating that information 'becomes known to the Recipient through observation' is not considered confidential. This in-text contradicts the original definition of Confidential Information, which broadly covers 'any information, technical data, or know-how' disclosed. If the recipient independently observes something related to the Discloser's business, it's now unclear if that information is protected, creating a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definition. "Confidential Information" means any information, technical data, or know - how, inc luding, but not limited to, that which relates to research, product plans, products, services, customers, markets, opt -in information including Short -code and URL information, marketing or finances, which Confidential Information is designated in writing t o be confidential or proprietary, or if given orally, is confirmed promptly in writing as having been disclosed as confidential or proprietary. Confidential Information does not include information, technical data or know -how which: (i) is in the possessio n of the receiving party at the time of disclosure as shown by the receiving party's files and records immediately prior to the time of disclosure; (ii) prior or after the time of disclosure becomes part of the public knowledge or literature, not as a resu lt of any inaction or action of the receiving party; (iii) is approved for release by the Discloser in writing; or (iv) becomes known to the Recipient through observation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds clause (iv), stating that information known to the recipient through observation is not considered Confidential Information. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information' as 'any information, technical data, or know-how'. This creates a contradiction because the recipient could observe something and, according to the changed text, that information is not confidential, even though the definition in the contradicted text is very broad and includes 'any information'. The original text, without clause (iv), did not create this contradiction, because it could be argued that all observed information fell under 'any information'. This directly conflicts with the broad definition, leading to an in-text contradiction as per the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees that it wil l take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized hereunder to have a ny such information, which measures shall include the highest degree of care that Recipient utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees that it wil l take commercially reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized hereunder to have a ny such information, which measures shall include the degree of care that Recipient utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'all reasonable measures' is replaced with 'commercially reasonable measures,' and 'highest degree of care' is changed to 'the degree of care'. The change creates an in-text contradiction: While the original wording obligated the Recipient to take the *highest* degree of care, the modification only requires them to use the *same* level of care as they would for their own confidential information. This creates ambiguity, as the recipient is no longer held to as high a standard of protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non -Disclosure of Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than that s et forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. The Recipient will not disclose any Confidential Information to third parties except those directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of Recipient who are required to have the information in order to car ry out the purpose set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. Recipient has had or will have directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of Recipient to whom Confidential Information is disclosed or who have access to Confidential Information sig n a Non -Disclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this Agreement and will promptly notify the Discloser in writing of the names of each such person who has signed such agreements after such agreements are signed. Recipient agrees that it wil l take commercially reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized hereunder to have a ny such information, which measures shall include the degree of care that Recipient utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. Recipient agrees to notify the Discloser in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of s uch Confidential Information which may come to its attention. Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA )
perturbation.justification: The original text required 'all reasonable measures' and the 'highest degree of care'. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'commercially reasonable measures' and 'the degree of care' that the Recipient uses for its own confidential information. Since the contradicted text contains the modified language, and the original intent was to hold the recipient to a higher standard than their own practices, the modified language within the contradicted text now fails to meet that higher standard, leading to an in-text contradiction because the level of care is no longer aligned with the initial agreement.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient will not disclose any Confidential Information to third parties except those directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of Recipient who are required to have the information in order to car ry out the purpose set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to its parent company, subsidiaries, and affiliates, regardless of whether those parties are required to have the information in order to carry out the purpose set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. The Recipient will not disclose any Confidential Information to third parties except those directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of Recipient who are required to have the information in order to car ry out the purpose set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction by allowing disclosure to specific related entities (parent company, subsidiaries, affiliates) without the 'need to know' restriction that applies to other third parties. This directly contradicts the subsequent clause that restricts disclosure to only those who 'are required to have the information.' The discrepancy creates uncertainty about the permissible scope of disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non -Disclosure of Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than that s et forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. The Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to its parent company, subsidiaries, and affiliates, regardless of whether those parties are required to have the information in order to carry out the purpose set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. The Recipient will not disclose any Confidential Information to third parties except those directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of Recipient who are required to have the information in order to car ry out the purpose set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. Recipient has had or will have directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of Recipient to whom Confidential Information is disclosed or who have access to Confidential Information sig n a Non -Disclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this Agreement and will promptly notify the Discloser in writing of the names of each such person who has signed such agreements after such agreements are signed. Recipient agrees that it wil l take commercially reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized hereunder to have a ny such information, which measures shall include the degree of care that Recipient utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. Recipient agrees to notify the Discloser in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of s uch Confidential Information which may come to its attention. Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA )
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating "The Recipient may disclose Confidential Information to its parent company, subsidiaries, and affiliates, regardless of whether those parties are required to have the information in order to carry out the purpose set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement." This directly conflicts with the later statement: "The Recipient will not disclose any Confidential Information to third parties except those directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of Recipient who are required to have the information in order to car ry out the purpose set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement." The first statement allows disclosure to related entities without a 'need to know' basis, while the second restricts disclosure to other third parties only to those with a 'need to know'. This creates an in-text contradiction within the same section and also between the original statement and the subsequent limitation.

perturbation.original_text: Any materials or documents of which have been furnished by the Discloser to the Recipient will be promptly returned, accompanied by copies of such doc umentation, after the evaluation set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement has been concluded.
perturbation.changed_text: Any materials or documents of which have been furnished by the Discloser to the Recipient will be promptly returned, without copies of such documentation, after the evaluation set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement has been concluded.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a direct in-text contradiction. Originally, the recipient must return the materials *with* copies. The changed text requires the recipient to return the materials *without* copies. This results in an unclear instruction regarding the handling of copies after evaluation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Materials. Any materials or documents of which have been furnished by the Discloser to the Recipient will be promptly returned, without copies of such documentation, after the evaluation set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement has been concluded.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that materials must be returned *with* copies, while the changed text requires returning materials *without* copies. The contradicted text also states materials must be returned *without* copies. The changed text, requiring the recipient to return materials 'without copies,' directly contradicts the original requirement of returning materials 'accompanied by copies'. The Contradicted text reinforces the contradiction introduced by the changed text
